steps.title: Recognize the elimination-then-endorsement pattern
steps.content: The passage systematically dismisses two explanations with “but in contrast” and “untenable,” then pivots with “Instead” to endorse a third. This three-part structure—ruled out, ruled out, endorsed—defines the text's movement.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content: Choice C maps this exactly: it identifies the phenomenon, the two refuted explanations with their specific weaknesses, and endorses the third as plausible. The other three choices either reframe what's being rejected (A), add unsupported details like “new evidence” (B), or contradict the conclusion (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
